title: D003: Implement infrastructure deployment automation E2E
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** S010 (CC-0014)

---

## Summary

Implement `make deploy-infra` and `make teardown-infra` Makefile targets that deploy the full infrastructure stack (cert-manager, OpenBao, ESO, MariaDB Operator, Memcached Operator, infrastructure CRs, ExternalSecrets) to a kind cluster using FluxCD with kustomize overlays for kind-specific resource sizing. Bootstrap OpenBao end-to-end (init-unseal, secret engines, auth, policies, bootstrap secrets) to validate the full secret chain through ESO. Create a Chainsaw E2E test at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` validating all components reach healthy state. Extend `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` with an `e2e-infra` job running the full stack test on every PR and push to main.

---

## Scope

**Included:**
- `make deploy-infra` target replacing stub S008 — installs FluxCD in kind, applies existing manifests via kustomize overlays in dependency order
- `make teardown-infra` target (new) — deletes the kind cluster
- `make install-test-deps` target replacing stub S002 — installs chainsaw, flux CLI, kind, helm prerequisites
- `make e2e` target replacing stub S002 — runs Chainsaw tests
- Orchestration script `hack/deploy-infra.sh` — creates kind cluster, runs `flux install`, applies kustomize overlays in two phases (base → infrastructure), runs OpenBao bootstrap, waits for health
- Cleanup script `hack/teardown-infra.sh` — `kind delete cluster`
- Kind cluster config `hack/kind-config.yaml` (single control-plane node)
- Kustomize overlays `deploy/kind/base/` and `deploy/kind/infrastructure/` — patches HelmReleases and CRs for reduced replicas and `standard` storage class
- Full OpenBao bootstrap invocation (init-unseal → setup-secret-engines → setup-auth → setup-policies → write-bootstrap-secrets)
- Chainsaw E2E test `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` — asserts readiness of all operators, CRs, ClusterIssuer, and ExternalSecret sync status
- GitHub Actions `e2e-infra` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` using `helm/kind-action`, `fluxcd/flux2/action`, SHA-pinned actions per existing CI conventions

**Excluded:**
- Operator deployment (keystone-operator, c5c3-operator) — separate features S017/S022, YAGNI
- Modification of existing `deploy/flux-system/` manifests — read-only reference, kind-specific differences handled via kustomize overlays in `deploy/kind/`
- Production HA validation (3-replica Galera, multi-node Raft) — belongs to S024 stress tests, YAGNI
- Image build or push — no operator images needed for infrastructure-only stack
- Separate workflow file — single `ci.yaml` with new job, path-filtered optimization deferred

---

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph MakeTargets["Makefile Targets"]
        ITD["make install-test-deps"]
        DI["make deploy-infra"]
        TI["make teardown-infra"]
        E2E["make e2e"]
    end

    subgraph DeployScript["hack/deploy-infra.sh"]
        K["1. kind create cluster"]
        FI["2. flux install"]
        NS["3. kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base"]
        WAIT1["4. Wait: HelmReleases Ready"]
        INF["5. kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/infrastructure"]
        WAIT2["6. Wait: OpenBao pods Ready"]
        BOOT["7. OpenBao bootstrap scripts"]
        WAIT3["8. Wait: ExternalSecrets Synced"]
    end

    subgraph FluxReconciliation["FluxCD Reconciles HelmReleases"]
        CM["cert-manager"]
        OB["OpenBao"]
        MO["MariaDB Operator"]
        ESO["ESO"]
        MCO["Memcached Operator"]
    end

    subgraph InfraCRs["Infrastructure CRs via Kustomize"]
        CI2["ClusterIssuer"]
        TLS["OpenBao TLS Certificate"]
        MDB["MariaDB CR 1 replica"]
        MC["Memcached CR 1 replica"]
        CSS["ClusterSecretStore"]
        ES["ExternalSecrets x3"]
    end

    subgraph KindOverlays["deploy/kind/ Kustomize Overlays"]
        OVB["base/ — patches HelmRelease replicas and storage"]
        OVI["infrastructure/ — patches CR replicas and storage class"]
    end

    DI --> K --> FI --> NS
    NS --> OVB
    OVB -->|"FluxCD"| FluxReconciliation
    CM --> OB & MO & ESO & MCO
    FluxReconciliation --> WAIT1
    WAIT1 --> INF
    INF --> OVI
    OVI --> InfraCRs
    InfraCRs --> WAIT2 --> BOOT --> WAIT3
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CI as GitHub Actions
    participant Kind as kind cluster
    participant Flux as FluxCD
    participant K8s as Kubernetes API
    participant OB as OpenBao
    participant CS as Chainsaw

    CI->>Kind: kind create cluster
    CI->>Flux: flux install
    CI->>K8s: kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base
    Flux->>K8s: Reconcile cert-manager HelmRelease
    Flux->>K8s: Reconcile openbao, mariadb-op, eso, memcached-op
    CI->>K8s: Wait HelmReleases Ready
    CI->>K8s: kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/infrastructure
    K8s-->>K8s: cert-manager issues OpenBao TLS cert
    CI->>K8s: Wait OpenBao pods Ready
    CI->>OB: init-unseal.sh
    CI->>OB: setup-secret-engines, auth, policies, secrets
    CI->>K8s: Wait ExternalSecrets Synced
    CI->>CS: chainsaw test infra-stack-health
    CS->>K8s: Assert all components healthy
    CS-->>CI: JUnit XML report
```

---

## Key Components

- **`Makefile` targets**: Replace stubs `deploy-infra` (S008), `install-test-deps` (S002), `e2e` (S002); add new `teardown-infra`; each delegates to scripts in `hack/`
- **`hack/deploy-infra.sh`**: Orchestration script — creates kind cluster, runs `flux install`, applies `deploy/kind/base/` kustomization (FluxCD reconciles HelmReleases), applies `deploy/kind/infrastructure/` kustomization (CRs + ESO resources), runs OpenBao bootstrap scripts from `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/`, includes `wait_for_ready()` helpers with configurable timeouts; follows existing shell conventions (`set -euo pipefail`, SPDX header)
- **`hack/teardown-infra.sh`**: Cleanup script — `kind delete cluster --name <cluster-name>`
- **`hack/kind-config.yaml`**: Single control-plane node, sufficient for CI runners (~7GB RAM, 2 vCPUs)
- **`deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml`**: Kustomize overlay referencing `../../flux-system/`, patches OpenBao HelmRelease to standalone mode (1 replica, HA disabled); other operators unchanged (single-replica by default or stateless)
- **`deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml`**: Kustomize overlay referencing `../../flux-system/infrastructure/`, patches MariaDB CR (1 replica, Galera disabled, MaxScale disabled, `standard` storage class), patches Memcached CR (1 replica)
- **`tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml`**: Chainsaw v1alpha2 test asserting: cert-manager Deployment Ready, OpenBao StatefulSet Ready, ESO Deployment Ready, MariaDB Operator Deployment Ready, Memcached Operator Deployment Ready, ClusterIssuer Ready condition, MariaDB CR Ready condition, Memcached CR Ready condition, ClusterSecretStore Valid condition, ExternalSecrets SecretSynced condition; uses extended assert timeout (~5min) for operator startup
- **`.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — `e2e-infra` job**: New job alongside existing `lint` and `test`; uses SHA-pinned `helm/kind-action` to create cluster, installs FluxCD, runs `make deploy-infra`, executes `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/infrastructure/`, uploads JUnit report as artifact; `timeout-minutes: 20`; follows existing CI conventions (SHA-pinned actions, `permissions: contents: read`, `concurrency` with cancel-in-progress on PRs)

stories.title: Platform engineer deploys the full infrastructure stack to a kind cluster with a single command
stories.want: to run `make deploy-infra` and have the full infrastructure stack (cert-manager, OpenBao, ESO, MariaDB Operator, Memcached Operator, infrastructure CRs, ExternalSecrets) deployed and healthy in a local kind cluster
stories.so_that: I can validate the complete infrastructure chain locally before pushing changes
criteria: `make deploy-infra` creates a kind cluster, installs FluxCD, applies base kustomization, applies infrastructure kustomization, runs OpenBao bootstrap, and waits for all components to be healthy
All five HelmReleases (cert-manager, openbao, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, memcached-operator) reach Ready=True status
OpenBao is initialized, unsealed, and has secret engines + auth + policies configured
All three ExternalSecrets (keystone-admin, keystone-db, mariadb-root-password) reach SecretSynced status
ClusterIssuer, MariaDB CR, and Memcached CR reach Ready condition
The script exits 0 on success and non-zero on any failure with a descriptive error message

stories.title: Platform engineer tears down the infrastructure stack
stories.want: to run `make teardown-infra` and have the kind cluster fully deleted
stories.so_that: I can clean up resources and free local machine resources after testing
criteria: `make teardown-infra` deletes the kind cluster by name
The command is idempotent — running it when no cluster exists succeeds silently
Script exits 0 on success

stories.title: Platform engineer installs E2E test dependencies
stories.want: to run `make install-test-deps` to install chainsaw, flux CLI, kind, and helm prerequisites
stories.so_that: I can set up my development environment for running E2E tests without manual tool installation
criteria: `make install-test-deps` installs chainsaw, flux CLI, kind, and kubectl if not already present
The script is idempotent — re-running it does not fail if tools are already installed
Installed tool versions are pinned for reproducibility

stories.title: Platform engineer runs E2E tests against the deployed infrastructure
stories.want: to run `make e2e` and execute all Chainsaw E2E tests against the running kind cluster
stories.so_that: I can validate that the infrastructure stack is healthy before merging changes
criteria: `make e2e` runs `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/`
The Chainsaw test produces JUnit XML reports in `_output/reports/`
Tests use extended assert timeouts suitable for operator startup (~5min)
Failing tests produce descriptive output indicating which component is unhealthy

stories.title: CI pipeline validates infrastructure stack health on every PR
stories.want: to run the full infrastructure deployment and Chainsaw E2E test automatically on every PR and push to main
stories.so_that: infrastructure regressions are caught before merge
criteria: A new `e2e-infra` job is added to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`
The job uses SHA-pinned `helm/kind-action` to create a kind cluster
The job installs FluxCD, runs `make deploy-infra`, then runs Chainsaw tests
JUnit XML reports are uploaded as workflow artifacts
The job has `timeout-minutes: 20` to account for operator startup time
The job follows existing CI conventions: SHA-pinned actions, `permissions: contents: read`, concurrency with cancel-in-progress on PRs

stories.title: Kustomize overlays reduce resource requirements for kind
stories.want: kind-specific kustomize overlays that patch production manifests to use single-replica, reduced-resource configurations suitable for a single-node kind cluster
stories.so_that: the infrastructure stack can run on a developer laptop or CI runner with limited resources (~7GB RAM, 2 vCPUs)
criteria: `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` references `../../flux-system/` and patches OpenBao HelmRelease to standalone mode (1 replica, HA disabled)
`deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` references `../../flux-system/infrastructure/` and patches MariaDB CR to 1 replica with Galera disabled, MaxScale disabled, `standard` storage class
Memcached CR is patched to 1 replica
Other operators (cert-manager, mariadb-operator, ESO, memcached-operator) remain unchanged as they are single-replica or stateless by default

stories.title: Chainsaw E2E test validates all infrastructure components reach healthy state
stories.want: a comprehensive Chainsaw test that asserts readiness of all deployed components
stories.so_that: any infrastructure regression is immediately detectable
criteria: Test file exists at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml`
Test asserts cert-manager Deployment has Ready replicas
Test asserts OpenBao StatefulSet has Ready replicas
Test asserts ESO Deployment has Ready replicas
Test asserts MariaDB Operator Deployment has Ready replicas
Test asserts Memcached Operator Deployment has Ready replicas
Test asserts ClusterIssuer has Ready condition
Test asserts MariaDB CR has Ready condition
Test asserts Memcached CR has Ready condition
Test asserts ClusterSecretStore has Valid condition
Test asserts all three ExternalSecrets have SecretSynced condition
Test uses `apiVersion: chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha2` matching existing convention

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `make deploy-infra` target that deploys the full infrastructure stack to a kind cluster in dependency order
requirements.rationale: Replaces the S008 stub with a working deployment target for local and CI validation

scenarios.name: Full stack deployment succeeds
scenarios.when: user runs `make deploy-infra` with docker running and no existing kind cluster
scenarios.then: a kind cluster is created, FluxCD is installed, all operators and infrastructure CRs are deployed, OpenBao is bootstrapped, and all components reach healthy state

scenarios.name: Deployment fails if docker is not running
scenarios.when: user runs `make deploy-infra` without docker daemon running
scenarios.then: the script exits non-zero with an error message indicating docker is not available
and_then: no partial resources are left behind

scenarios.name: Deployment fails if cluster already exists
scenarios.when: user runs `make deploy-infra` and a kind cluster with the same name already exists
scenarios.then: the script exits non-zero with an error message suggesting `make teardown-infra` first
and_then: the existing cluster is not modified

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `make teardown-infra` target that deletes the kind cluster
requirements.rationale: Provides clean resource cleanup after testing

scenarios.name: Teardown deletes existing cluster
scenarios.when: user runs `make teardown-infra` with an existing kind cluster
scenarios.then: the kind cluster is fully deleted

scenarios.name: Teardown is idempotent when no cluster exists
scenarios.when: user runs `make teardown-infra` with no existing kind cluster
scenarios.then: the script exits 0 without error

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide kustomize overlays in `deploy/kind/` that patch production manifests for single-node kind cluster resource constraints
requirements.rationale: Production manifests use 3-replica HA configurations too heavy for kind; overlays enable local testing

scenarios.name: Base overlay patches OpenBao to standalone mode
scenarios.when: kustomize builds `deploy/kind/base/`
scenarios.then: the OpenBao HelmRelease is patched to standalone mode with 1 replica and HA disabled

scenarios.name: Infrastructure overlay patches MariaDB to single replica
scenarios.when: kustomize builds `deploy/kind/infrastructure/`
scenarios.then: the MariaDB CR uses 1 replica, Galera disabled, MaxScale disabled, `standard` storage class

scenarios.name: Overlays produce valid YAML
scenarios.when: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` and `kustomize build deploy/kind/infrastructure/` are run
scenarios.then: both commands produce valid Kubernetes YAML without errors

requirements.description: The deployment script SHALL apply manifests in two phases with health waits between phases
requirements.rationale: Infrastructure CRs depend on operator CRDs which are only available after phase 1 HelmReleases install their charts

scenarios.name: Phase 1 deploys operators before phase 2 CRs
scenarios.when: the deploy script runs
scenarios.then: it applies `deploy/kind/base/` first, waits for all HelmReleases to be Ready, then applies `deploy/kind/infrastructure/`
and_then: the phase ordering ensures CRDs exist before CRD-dependent resources are applied

scenarios.name: Script waits for OpenBao pods before bootstrapping
scenarios.when: phase 2 completes and OpenBao pods are starting
scenarios.then: the script waits for OpenBao StatefulSet pods to be Ready before running bootstrap scripts
and_then: bootstrap scripts do not fail due to unreachable pods

scenarios.name: Script waits for ExternalSecrets after bootstrap
scenarios.when: OpenBao bootstrap completes
scenarios.then: the script waits for all ExternalSecrets to reach SecretSynced status
and_then: the full secret chain from OpenBao through ESO to Kubernetes Secrets is validated

requirements.description: The deployment script SHALL invoke existing OpenBao bootstrap scripts from `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/` for full end-to-end secret chain validation
requirements.rationale: Reuses the idempotent bootstrap scripts created in CC-0009 rather than duplicating logic

scenarios.name: Bootstrap runs all five scripts in order
scenarios.when: the deploy script reaches the OpenBao bootstrap phase
scenarios.then: it runs init-unseal, setup-secret-engines, setup-auth, setup-policies, and write-bootstrap-secrets in sequence
and_then: the root token is extracted and passed to subsequent scripts

scenarios.name: Bootstrap adapts for kind single-node OpenBao
scenarios.when: OpenBao is running in standalone mode (1 replica) in kind
scenarios.then: init-unseal initializes and unseals only openbao-0 (not 3 pods)
and_then: the script does not error on missing pods 1 and 2

scenarios.name: Bootstrap failure stops deployment
scenarios.when: any bootstrap script fails
scenarios.then: the deploy script exits non-zero immediately
and_then: the error message indicates which bootstrap step failed

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `make install-test-deps` target that installs E2E test dependencies
requirements.rationale: Replaces the S002 stub with a working target for CI and developer use

scenarios.name: Install-test-deps installs required tools
scenarios.when: user runs `make install-test-deps`
scenarios.then: chainsaw, flux CLI, kind, and kubectl are installed with pinned versions

scenarios.name: Install-test-deps is idempotent
scenarios.when: user runs `make install-test-deps` with tools already installed
scenarios.then: the script completes without error and does not re-download already installed tools

requirements.description: The system SHALL provide a `make e2e` target that runs Chainsaw E2E tests
requirements.rationale: Replaces the S002 stub with a working E2E test runner

scenarios.name: E2E target runs Chainsaw tests
scenarios.when: user runs `make e2e` with a healthy infrastructure stack deployed
scenarios.then: `chainsaw test` is invoked with the project's chainsaw-config.yaml and produces JUnit reports
and_then: exit code matches test results (0 for pass, non-zero for fail)

scenarios.name: E2E target fails when no cluster is available
scenarios.when: user runs `make e2e` without a running kind cluster
scenarios.then: the command exits non-zero with an appropriate error

requirements.description: The Chainsaw E2E test SHALL assert readiness of all deployed infrastructure components
requirements.rationale: Validates the full infrastructure stack is healthy — the primary deliverable of this feature

scenarios.name: All operator Deployments have Ready replicas
scenarios.when: the Chainsaw test runs after a successful deployment
scenarios.then: it asserts cert-manager, ESO, MariaDB Operator, and Memcached Operator Deployments have available replicas > 0

scenarios.name: OpenBao StatefulSet is Ready
scenarios.when: the Chainsaw test runs
scenarios.then: it asserts the OpenBao StatefulSet has readyReplicas >= 1

scenarios.name: Infrastructure CRs have Ready conditions
scenarios.when: the Chainsaw test runs
scenarios.then: it asserts ClusterIssuer has Ready=True, MariaDB CR has Ready condition, Memcached CR has Ready condition

scenarios.name: ESO resources are functional
scenarios.when: the Chainsaw test runs
scenarios.then: it asserts ClusterSecretStore has Valid condition and all three ExternalSecrets have SecretSynced condition

requirements.description: The GitHub Actions CI workflow SHALL include an `e2e-infra` job that runs the infrastructure E2E test on every PR and push to main
requirements.rationale: Catches infrastructure regressions in CI before merge

scenarios.name: E2E-infra job runs on PR
scenarios.when: a pull request is opened or updated targeting main
scenarios.then: the `e2e-infra` job runs the full infrastructure deployment and Chainsaw E2E test
and_then: JUnit XML report is uploaded as a workflow artifact

scenarios.name: E2E-infra job uses SHA-pinned actions
scenarios.when: the workflow is inspected
scenarios.then: all `uses:` references use SHA-pinned versions (not floating tags) matching the existing CI convention

scenarios.name: E2E-infra job respects timeout
scenarios.when: the job runs
scenarios.then: it has `timeout-minutes: 20` to allow for operator startup time
and_then: the job is cancelled if it exceeds the timeout

requirements.description: The kind cluster configuration SHALL define a single control-plane node sufficient for CI runners
requirements.rationale: Minimises resource requirements while providing a functional Kubernetes cluster

scenarios.name: Kind config creates single-node cluster
scenarios.when: `kind create cluster --config hack/kind-config.yaml` is run
scenarios.then: a single control-plane node cluster is created
and_then: the cluster is functional and can run FluxCD and all operators

requirements.description: All new shell scripts SHALL follow existing project conventions: `set -euo pipefail`, SPDX Apache-2.0 header, feature ID reference
requirements.rationale: Consistency with existing scripts in deploy/openbao/bootstrap/ and tests/scripts/

scenarios.name: Scripts have SPDX header and feature reference
scenarios.when: new shell scripts are inspected
scenarios.then: each script starts with the SPDX Apache-2.0 header and references CC-0010 feature ID

scenarios.name: Scripts use strict mode
scenarios.when: new shell scripts are inspected
scenarios.then: each script includes `set -euo pipefail` near the top

requirements.description: The deployment script SHOULD include configurable timeouts with wait_for_ready helper functions
requirements.rationale: Operators may take varying amounts of time to start depending on the environment; configurable timeouts prevent premature failures and allow tuning

scenarios.name: Default timeouts are sufficient for CI
scenarios.when: the deploy script runs with default timeout values
scenarios.then: the default timeouts (e.g., 300s for HelmRelease readiness, 300s for pod readiness, 120s for ExternalSecret sync) are sufficient for GitHub Actions runners

scenarios.name: Timeouts are overridable via environment variables
scenarios.when: the deploy script is run with `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT=600` environment variable
scenarios.then: the script uses the overridden timeout value

tasks.title: Create kind cluster config `hack/kind-config.yaml` — single control-plane node with standard settings for CI runners (REQ-010, REQ-011)

Create kustomize overlay `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` — references `../../flux-system/`, adds strategic merge patch for OpenBao HelmRelease (standalone mode: 1 replica, HA disabled, standalone Raft config without retry_join peers, `standard` storage class). Validate with `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` (REQ-003)

Create kustomize overlay `deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` — references `../../flux-system/infrastructure/`, adds strategic merge patches for MariaDB CR (1 replica, Galera disabled, MaxScale disabled, `standard` storage class) and Memcached CR (1 replica). Validate with `kustomize build deploy/kind/infrastructure/` (REQ-003)

Create orchestration script `hack/deploy-infra.sh` — implements the full 8-step deployment sequence: kind create cluster (using hack/kind-config.yaml), flux install, kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base, wait for HelmReleases Ready, kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/infrastructure, wait for OpenBao pods Ready, run OpenBao bootstrap, wait for ExternalSecrets synced. Includes `log()`, `wait_for_helmreleases()`, `wait_for_pods()`, `wait_for_externalsecrets()` helper functions with configurable timeouts via environment variables. SPDX header, `set -euo pipefail`, CC-0010 reference. Handles pre-flight checks (docker running, no existing cluster). (REQ-001, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-011, REQ-012)

Create teardown script `hack/teardown-infra.sh` — runs `kind delete cluster --name <cluster-name>`, idempotent (succeeds even if cluster does not exist). SPDX header, `set -euo pipefail`, CC-0010 reference. (REQ-002, REQ-011)

Create install-test-deps script `hack/install-test-deps.sh` — installs pinned versions of chainsaw, flux CLI, kind, and kubectl. Skips download if tool already exists at correct version. SPDX header, `set -euo pipefail`, CC-0010 reference. (REQ-006, REQ-011)

Update Makefile: replace `deploy-infra` stub (S008) with `hack/deploy-infra.sh`, add `teardown-infra` target delegating to `hack/teardown-infra.sh`, replace `install-test-deps` stub (S002) with `hack/install-test-deps.sh`, replace `e2e` stub (S002) with `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/`. Keep existing .PHONY declarations and comment style. (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-006, REQ-007)

Create Chainsaw E2E test `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` — v1alpha2 Test with steps asserting: (1) cert-manager Deployment readiness in cert-manager namespace, (2) OpenBao StatefulSet readiness in openbao-system, (3) ESO Deployment readiness in external-secrets, (4) MariaDB Operator Deployment readiness in mariadb-system, (5) Memcached Operator Deployment readiness in memcached-system, (6) ClusterIssuer Ready condition on selfsigned-cluster-issuer, (7) MariaDB CR Ready condition on openstack-db, (8) Memcached CR Ready condition on openstack-memcached, (9) ClusterSecretStore Valid condition on openbao-cluster-store, (10) ExternalSecret SecretSynced for keystone-admin, keystone-db, mariadb-root-password. Uses extended assert timeout (~5min). SPDX header, CC-0010 reference. (REQ-008)

Add `e2e-infra` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — new job alongside existing `lint` and `test` (no `needs:` dependency). Steps: checkout (SHA-pinned actions/checkout), setup-go (SHA-pinned actions/setup-go with go-version-file: go.work), create kind cluster (SHA-pinned helm/kind-action with config: hack/kind-config.yaml), install flux CLI, run `make deploy-infra`, run `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/infrastructure/`, upload JUnit report as artifact (SHA-pinned actions/upload-artifact). `timeout-minutes: 20`, follows existing `permissions: contents: read` and concurrency conventions. (REQ-009)

Write reference documentation `docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md` — documents `make deploy-infra`, `make teardown-infra`, `make install-test-deps`, `make e2e` targets; deployment sequence diagram; kustomize overlay structure in `deploy/kind/`; environment variables for timeout configuration; prerequisites (docker, kubectl); CI job description. (REQ-001 through REQ-012)

test_specifications.story: Kustomize overlays reduce resource requirements for kind
test_specifications.expected: `kustomize build deploy/kind/infrastructure/` produces valid YAML with MariaDB CR at 1 replica and Memcached CR at 1 replica

test_specifications.expected: The rendered OpenBao HelmRelease has `server.ha.enabled: false`, `server.ha.replicas: 1`, and no retry_join peers in the Raft config

test_specifications.expected: The rendered MariaDB CR has `replicas: 1`, `galera.enabled: false`, `maxScale.enabled: false`, `storage.storageClassName: standard`

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys the full infrastructure stack to a kind cluster with a single command
test_specifications.expected: After kind cluster creation, `flux install` is run and flux-system pods are running

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer deploys the full infrastructure stack to a kind cluster with a single command
test_specifications.expected: After applying base kustomization, the script waits until all 5 HelmReleases (cert-manager, openbao, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, memcached-operator) show Ready=True

test_specifications.expected: After OpenBao pods are Ready, the script invokes all 5 bootstrap scripts (init-unseal, setup-secret-engines, setup-auth, setup-policies, write-bootstrap-secrets) in sequence

test_specifications.story: Platform engineer tears down the infrastructure stack

review_criteria: All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-011) have corresponding implementation — no stubs
`kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` and `kustomize build deploy/kind/infrastructure/` produce valid YAML with correct patches (OpenBao standalone, MariaDB 1 replica, Memcached 1 replica)
The full `make deploy-infra` then `make e2e` then `make teardown-infra` cycle completes successfully in a clean environment
Chainsaw E2E test validates all 11 component health checks (5 operators, ClusterIssuer, MariaDB CR, Memcached CR, ClusterSecretStore, 3 ExternalSecrets)
All shell scripts follow project conventions: SPDX header, `set -euo pipefail`, CC-0010 feature reference, log() function with timestamps
GitHub Actions e2e-infra job uses SHA-pinned actions consistent with existing lint and test jobs, timeout-minutes: 20, uploads JUnit report
Kustomize overlays do not modify production manifests — all kind-specific differences are in `deploy/kind/` patches only
OpenBao bootstrap scripts from CC-0009 are invoked as-is without modification — kind-specific adaptations handled in deploy-infra.sh (e.g., PODS variable override for standalone mode)
Reference documentation covers all Makefile targets, deployment sequence, environment variables, and prerequisites
implementation_notes:
**Architecture**: This feature creates a shell-based deployment orchestration layer on top of the existing FluxCD manifests (CC-0008) and OpenBao bootstrap scripts (CC-0009). The key design choice is using kustomize overlays in `deploy/kind/` to patch production manifests rather than creating separate kind-specific manifests — this ensures kind testing always validates the same manifests that ship to production, with only resource sizing differences.

**Kustomize overlay strategy**: `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` uses the existing `deploy/flux-system/` as its base and adds a strategic merge patch for the OpenBao HelmRelease. The patch must set `server.ha.enabled: false`, `server.ha.replicas: 1`, change the Raft config to standalone mode (removing retry_join peers), and set `dataStorage.storageClass: standard`. Similarly, `deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` patches MariaDB (1 replica, no Galera, no MaxScale, `standard` storage class) and Memcached (1 replica).

**OpenBao bootstrap adaptation**: The existing bootstrap scripts (CC-0009) expect 3-replica HA. The deploy-infra.sh script must adapt by setting `PODS=(openbao-0)` to skip pods 1 and 2 during init-unseal. The BAO_TOKEN extraction from the init-unseal output must be passed to subsequent scripts. Since the bootstrap scripts use `kubectl exec` with the namespace from common.sh, no modifications to the CC-0009 scripts are needed.

**Deployment sequence**: The 8-step sequence is: (1) kind create cluster, (2) flux install, (3) kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base, (4) wait HelmReleases Ready, (5) kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/infrastructure, (6) wait OpenBao pods Ready, (7) OpenBao bootstrap (5 scripts), (8) wait ExternalSecrets synced. The wait functions use `kubectl wait` with configurable timeouts defaulting to 300s.

**CI job**: The e2e-infra job runs independently of lint and test (no `needs:` dependency). It uses `helm/kind-action` to create the cluster (this action handles docker setup in GitHub runners), installs FluxCD via the flux CLI, then delegates to `make deploy-infra` and `chainsaw test`. The JUnit report is uploaded via `actions/upload-artifact`.

**Key files to reference**: `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` (base resources), `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` (infrastructure CRs), `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/*.sh` (bootstrap scripts from CC-0009), `deploy/flux-system/releases/openbao.yaml` (OpenBao HelmRelease to patch), `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml` (MariaDB CR to patch), `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/memcached.yaml` (Memcached CR to patch), `tests/e2e/keystone/invalid-cr/chainsaw-test.yaml` (Chainsaw test pattern reference), `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` (CI conventions reference).

**Potential pitfalls**: (1) FluxCD reconciliation timing — HelmReleases may take 2-3 minutes to become Ready after apply; the wait timeout must account for this. (2) OpenBao TLS — the cert-manager Certificate for OpenBao TLS must be issued before OpenBao pods can start; this is handled by the phase ordering. (3) OpenBao standalone Raft — standalone mode requires the Raft config to omit retry_join peers, otherwise the single node will fail to bootstrap. (4) kind networking — kind uses a custom Docker network; FluxCD's source-controller must be able to reach external Helm registries from within the cluster.
